# Get CI relationships

**Connector:** [SDP - CMDB (v1 legacy)](../README.md) (`sdp-cmdb-v1`) · **Action index:** [all actions](README.md)
**Endpoint:** `GET /api/cmdb/cirelationships/{ci_name}` · **Operation ID:** `GetCIRelationships`

Gets all relationships of one CI, addressed **by CI name** (legacy V1 API).

## Parameters

| Designer label | Name | Where | Type | Required |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| CI Name | `ci_name` | URL path | string | yes |

### CI Name (`ci_name`)

The configuration item's **name**, exactly as SDP shows it — **case-sensitive**. (V1 addresses CIs by name, not ID.)

## Authentication

The connection sends the technician key on every call as the `TECHNICIAN_KEY` **query
parameter** (secured connection parameter; same secret value as the V3 `authtoken`). Because
it travels in the URL, **HTTPS is mandatory** and the key should be scoped tightly — see the
[connector README](../README.md#whats-different-about-v1).

## Response

Success (200) returns the `V1Envelope` shape:

- `API` — object

V1 responses are JSON with the legacy envelope
`{ "API": { "response": { "operation": { "result": {...}, "Details": ... } } } }`.
Success is `result.statuscode` **200** (with `result.status` `"Success"`); errors put the
reason in `result.message`. This is *not* the V3 `response_status` shape.

Anything else comes back on the `default` response — V1 failures put the reason in `result.message`, and V3-style rejections can also appear.

## Notes, limits & verification

- Modeled from the official API contract and a working client; live verification on a matching build is still pending.
- The V1 API also supports filtering by relationship type and finding the relationship **between two CIs** via sub-paths (`cirelationships/{ci}/{relationship_type}`, `cirelationships/{ci}<->{other}`) — not exposed as separate actions here because of the awkward path characters; see the [connector README](../README.md).
- On 15100+ builds (like the public demo) this endpoint is superseded by V3, so it could not be live-verified there — verify on a real sub-15100 instance.
